D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Bouba/Kiki — Cross-Modal Sound-Shape Mapping

~95% of people pair the round blob shape with "bouba" and the spiky shape with "kiki" — universal cross-modal correspondence; colour pairs (yellow→light, deep red→heavy) follow similar logic

Ramachandran & Hubbard (2001) re-popularised Köhler's 1929 Maluma/Takete demo. The phoneme shape (open /b/ vs angular /k/) maps to visual smoothness. Marketing exploits this: product names like "Cocoa Roundies" lean soft; "Krunchies" lean angular.

Toggle name and the rounded/angular shape attribution.
